summary: Tight set with Picasso Moon and stellar Eyes‑Est‑Terrapin
blurb: The band delivers a tight, energetic performance highlighted by Picasso Moon, the Eyes > Estimated Prophet > Terrapin Station suite, and Black Peter. Jerry’s adventurous solo work and vigorous drumming keep the momentum high throughout both sets.
review: October 30, 1991 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum captures a surprisingly tight snapshot of the early‑’90s Dead. The first set opens with a punchy Picasso Moon that immediately signals the night’s energy, followed by a crisp run through classics like Friend Of The Devil and The Music Never Stopped. The second set is where the show truly shines: Eyes Of The World flows straight into Estimated Prophet and then Terrapin Station, creating a seamless “Eyes‑Est‑Terrapin” sequence that reviewers call “immaculate.” Black Peter closes the set on a high note before the band rolls into an upbeat Around & Around and a rousing Mighty Quinn encore. Across all recordings Jerry is described as “scuba diving,” delivering adventurous leads, while Phil’s bass work shows renewed vigor. Both Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick lock in with each other, driving the keyboards forward, and Mickey and Bill keep the percussion tight and propulsive.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive and one is mixed; no negative consensus appears. Reviewers repeatedly praise the tight ensemble, standout sequences (Picasso Moon, Eyes‑Est‑Terrapin), and energetic playing, justifying a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Immaculate DSBD source with crystal‑clear balance; captures the tight playing and standout songs without dropouts.

key_highlights: Tight ensemble cohesion across both sets
Standout Picasso Moon opening and Black Peter finale
"Eyes > Estimated Prophet > Terrapin Station" sequence praised as immaculate
Vibrant drumming from Mickey and Bill adds drive

band_performance.Jerry: Described as "scuba diving Jerry," delivering adventurous, energetic solos throughout the show.
band_performance.Phil: Shows renewed vigor (referred to as Bobby/Philly) with strong, propulsive bass lines.
band_performance.Bob: Displays newly found vigor that meshes well with the overall tightness of the band.
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick are in sync, driving the keyboard textures forward and adding depth to the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides vigorous, tight percussion that fuels the band's momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers equally tight drumming, complementing Mickey and keeping the rhythmic foundation solid.
